task_yaml.task:
Please open this YouTube video:
`https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=39ColarOWKo`

If the YouTube page is temporarily unavailable, use this backup video with the same content instead:
`https://www.dailymotion.com/video/x9rlvem`
The backup video must also let you actually play the full video. Do not rely only on a subtitle page,
clip list, or text summary; choose meme moments based on footage you actually viewed.

In this video, find the segment where a groundhog / woodchuck steals food and eats it in front of
the surveillance camera.
Do not guess from subtitles or summaries only; your answer must be based on the video footage you actually saw.

After finding the segment, identify every distinct food item that the groundhog eats or holds while eating
in that segment.
For each food item, in the order it appears in the video, capture 1 original key frame that best shows
both the food and the groundhog, then create 1 meme image from that same key frame.

Then use Python (Pillow) to add meme text to each image:
- Large white text with an outline
- Prefer placing the text at the bottom of the image; if the bottom would cover the subject, place it
  somewhere else that does not obscure the groundhog or the food

Output requirements:
1. Save the original key frame for each food item to `/tmp_workspace/results/groundhog_frames/`
2. Save the finished meme image for each food item to `/tmp_workspace/results/memes/`
3. Also generate `/tmp_workspace/results/meme_index.md`, listing one row per item in video order:
   - Index number
   - Food name / type
   - Video timestamp (required, in a format such as `mm:ss` or `hh:mm:ss`)
   - Original key frame filename
   - Finished meme filename
   - Scene description
   - The meme caption you wrote
